sources.notes: Used to confirm the transformation to x -> x-1 or floor(x/2), the trailing-zero characterization of zero Grundy states, and interval counting.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Determine the winner of the conditional version of the same impartial-game sum, where each series can be normalized to a one-dimensional state.
annotation.constraints: Large value intervals require counting Grundy classes over ranges in logarithmic time.
annotation.core_idea: Normalize a series by d=b-a and replace an upper bound x with x'=floor((x-a)/d). The game becomes a one-dimensional impartial game with moves x -> x-1 and x -> floor(x/2), ending at x=2. Its Grundy numbers have a bit-pattern characterization: g(x)=0 exactly when x has an odd number of trailing zero bits, while nonzero values alternate between 1 and 2. Count how many values in a transformed interval produce each Grundy value, adjust for boundary blocks with different multiplicities in the original interval, and XOR the parity of counts across all series.
annotation.complexity: O(n log V) per test case.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Transform each original interval to quotient states, count parities of Grundy 0/1/2 over the quotient interval, and XOR the resulting contribution.

tags.evidence: The solution computes Grundy values for each independent game and XORs their range contributions.
The zero Grundy states are characterized by the parity of the trailing-zero count in binary.
The original arithmetic-progression state is reduced to the quotient x'=floor((x-a)/d).
The nonzero Grundy values alternate, allowing range contributions to be counted by parity.
The problem is an impartial game winner decision after normalization.
